title: The Blind and Mute Man
subtitle: A man bound by a demon, blind and mute, is brought to Jesus and made whole

stillness: He who knows God but does not confess Him
speaks with the body, yet in the soul is silent.
Let your soul not be silent now.
gospel: there was brought to the Lord
so that he saw and spoke.
Three signs were wrought at once in the man:
the blind sees, the mute speaks,
the one possessed by the demon is set free.
And all the crowds were amazed, and said:
Is not this the Son of David?
behold: See the man brought before the Lord:
and had made him blind and mute,
and, as Chrysostom says, deaf as well.
The malice of the demon abounded greatly in him.
But the mercy of God abounded over him:
Jesus cast the demon out of the one it had seized,
The malice of the demon abounds;
the divine mercy abounds beyond it.
consider: Understand: that man is yourself.
Spiritually, he has a demon who persists in mortal sin.
For as long as sin reigns in a man,
so long is he held conquered and captive, possessed by the devil.
And a threefold demon torments him:
pride, in the mind;
concupiscence, in the flesh;
covetousness, in things he owns.
This threefold demon makes him mute.
For speech was given to man to praise God and give thanks,
to speak the truth and build up his neighbor,
to confess his sin and beg pardon for it;
and pride, avarice, and lust tear all this speech away.
It makes him blind as well,
so that he does not see what belongs to his salvation,
and does not follow the Light that says: I am the light of the world,
but walks in darkness.
Yet this demoniac, blind and mute,
if he be offered to the Lord, if he turn to penance,
at once Jesus will heal him,
so that, the demon cast out, he may see and speak.
prayer: the devil I led into the house of my soul:
drive him out, and dwell there Yourself.
Open my tongue, that I may praise You,
and accuse myself before You in confession.
Restore my light, that I may see behind me
my sins, how foul, how many, how grievous, and grieve;
and see before me Your judgment to come, and fear;
and unseal my hearing, that I may hear You
Cast out the demon, and let me see and speak.

latinJewel.latin: Abundat dæmonis malitia; superabundat divina clementia.
latinJewel.english: The malice of the demon abounds; the divine mercy abounds beyond it.
